prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
In the Year of Our Lord’s ledgers, in the Third Quarter, I was hired by the House of Finance to set down the marvels of their vaporous engine called SaaS, which dwelleth in a Cloud no mule may reach.  
They say their Monthly Recurring Revenue, a tithe drawn from distant “user seats,” waxed by seventeen parts in the hundred since the last reckoning, though 3.2% of their paying vassals did flee the manor and were sorrowfully inked upon the churn roll.  
Great banners were also raised for Net Revenue Retention at one hundred twenty-nine in the hundred, for by esoteric upsell rites a hamlet of nine seats became twenty, and the uptime oracle vowed the portcullis stood open 99.98% of days, save when a dragon named us-east-1 took a coughing fit.  
Yet I remain sore perplexed, for they brandish relics called Pipeline Health, CAC-to-LTV, and EBITDA, swear the treasury hearth burneth 1.2 million gold each moon with runway for twenty-one, and preach that salvation abideth in a free tier where 27% of pilgrims convert by clicking, though I can find neither loom nor plough to prove how this Dashboard minteth coin.
